title: Plane Task Definition Schema
description: Formal schema for every Plane work item in the agent-factory project. Each task is a hypothesis about which factory assets best solve the defined problem.

requirements.description: Detailed requirements in HTML format. Must be embedded in the Plane issue description under an '## Requirements' heading.

acceptance_criteria.description: Measurable acceptance criteria in HTML format. Must appear under '## Acceptance Criteria' heading.

workflows.description: Workflow slugs from .agent/workflows/ to apply. These are the SOPs that orchestrate agent actions for this task. Use [NEW] prefix for workflows to be created.

agents.description: Agent identifiers from .agent/agents/ or memory MCP (Agent Team Registry). These are the specialist personas executing the task. Use [NEW] prefix for agents to be created.

skills.description: Skill names from .agent/skills/ (Tier 3 bundles). These are the atomic tactical procedures required. Use [NEW] prefix for skills to be created.

scripts.description: Script paths from scripts/ to be used or created. Include validation, maintenance, and utility scripts. Use [NEW] prefix for scripts to be created.

knowledge.description: Knowledge references. Includes .agent/knowledge/ JSON files AND memory MCP entities. These two sources MUST be kept in sync — the memory graph is the live index, and the knowledge files are the persisted evidence layer.

patterns.description: Design/implementation patterns from .agent/patterns/ (Tier 1). Structural blueprints/schemas for agents and skills. Use [NEW] prefix for patterns to be created.

templates.description: Template files from .agent/templates/ (Tier 2). Generative skeletons and seeds for project/skill creation. Use [NEW] prefix for templates to be created.

blueprints.description: Blueprint references from .agent/blueprints/ (Foundational layer). Architectural templates for project generation. Use [NEW] prefix for blueprints to be created.

tests.description: Test strategy. Each test makes the task hypothesis falsifiable — if tests pass, the hypothesis is confirmed.

rules.description: Agent rules from .agent/rules/ that govern execution of this task. Query memory MCP 'Agent_Rules_Index' for quick orientation.

memory_queries.description: Memory MCP entity names to query for fast context before starting work. Key entities: 'System_Consciousness', 'Skill Catalog', 'Agent Team Registry', 'Pattern Catalog', 'Template Catalog', 'Blueprint Catalog'.

AssetRef.description: Reference to an existing factory asset (e.g., 'managing-plane-tasks') or a new asset to be created as a deliverable (e.g., '[NEW] task-schema-validation'). The [NEW] prefix signals that this asset does not yet exist and must be built during task execution.

name.description: Knowledge item name. Can be a .agent/knowledge/ filename or a memory MCP entity name.

source.description: Where the knowledge lives. 'knowledge_file' = .agent/knowledge/ JSON, 'memory_mcp' = memory MCP graph entity, 'external' = external source (URL, book, API docs). Both knowledge_file and memory_mcp MUST be kept in sync.

is_new.description: If true, this knowledge item must be created as part of the task.

script.description: Path to the test script (relative to project root).

command.description: Exact CLI command to run the test. Must use 'conda run -p D:\Anaconda\envs\cursor-factory' for Python.

expected.description: Description of what a passing test looks like.

examples.requirements:
R1: Formal Task Definition Schema

Define a JSON schema...

tests.command: conda run -p D:\Anaconda\envs\cursor-factory python -m pytest tests/test_task_schema.py -v

tests.expected: Create a task using the schema and verify it renders correctly in Plane
